title: Heart Disease Chatbot
content:
Table of Contents Introduction Proposed Model Overview System Architecture Agent interaction Installation Evaluation Introduction In this project undertaken for the Introduction to Machine Learning and Data Mining course, we address the critical issue of providing accurate and timely health information on cardiovascular diseases. Utilizing the power of Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing, we aim to develop a conversational query model that simplifies access to vital information for users, potentially improving health outcomes and awareness.
Proposed Model Overview Our approach is based on the Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) model, which combines the strengths of document retrieval and generative language models to provide precise answers.
The system architecture integrates a Document Retrieval Module for identifying relevant medical texts (I crawl in Cleveland Clinic ), a Text Retrieval Module that extracts pertinent information, and a Large Language Model (LLM) that synthesizes and delivers user-friendly responses. This model is designed to leverage extensive datasets and pre-trained language models, ensuring high accuracy and relevance in the health advisory domain.
System Architecture The model proposed in the project is a conversational query model that is improved based on the RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) model. This model has the task of retrieving relevant information from the knowledge base to answer user queries. The project model consists of three main components.
Documents retriever: Once the user asks, the model uses the Rank-BM25 library to evaluate and rank the similarity between the user’s query content and the content of 1000 documents in knowledge base. The model then selects the most relevant documents. Chunk retriever: The chunk retriever takes the text chunks that are semantically related to the user’s query from the documents selected from the document retriever. These text chunks are then used to create the context that is fed into the large language model to generate the answer. Large language model (LLM): The large language model uses the context to generate the answer. The project uses three pre-trained large language models: llama-2-7b, mistral-7b and zephyr-7b to compare the results. Agent interaction Installation To install this project, follow these steps:
Clone the repository git clone https://github.com/Dodero10/Heart-Disease-Chatbot.git Set up a environment with Python 3.10 conda create -n chatbot python=3.10 conda activate chatbot pip install -r requirements.txt Download the models ✅ Llama2-7b ✅ Mistral-7b ✅ Zephyr-7b Usage
Start the Chatbot:
streamlit run app.py Demo
Evaluation We evaluate the RAG model based on three aspects:
Faithfulness: refers to the idea that the answer should be grounded in the given context. This is important to ensure that the retrieved context can act as a justification for the generated answer. Answer Relevance: refers to the idea that the generated answer should address the actual question that was provided. Context Relevance: refers to the idea that the retrieved context should be focused, containing as little irrelevant information as possible.

title: Sentiment Analysis
content:
Table of Contents Introduction Project Objectives Models Used BERT-based Model PhoBERT Mamba Bi-LSTM and Bi-GRU Data Utilized English Dataset Vietnamese Dataset Data Preprocessing Evaluation Results and Analysis Model Performance Comparison Conclusion Introduction Sentiment Analysis is a crucial field in natural language processing (NLP) that focuses on identifying and categorizing opinions expressed in text. This project aims to leverage advanced machine learning models to enhance the accuracy of sentiment classification for Vietnamese.
Project Objectives The main objectives of this Sentiment Analysis project are:
Evaluate the Effectiveness of Various Models:
Compare the performance of BERT-based models, PhoBERT, Mamba, and a combination of Bi-LSTM and Bi-GRU. Assess these models on both English and Vietnamese datasets in the context of Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (ABSA). Data Preprocessing and Model Optimization:
Implement data cleaning, tokenization, aspect labeling, and normalization techniques. Optimize models to enhance sentiment classification accuracy. Results Analysis:
Compare the results across different models. Analyze the strengths and weaknesses of each model in accurately detecting and classifying sentiments based on specific aspects. Models Used BERT-based Model The BERT-based model is utilized for English text sentiment analysis. It is known for its ability to understand the context of a word based on its surrounding words, providing high accuracy in sentiment classification tasks.
PhoBERT PhoBERT, a variant of BERT specifically pre-trained for Vietnamese, is employed for Vietnamese text sentiment analysis. It leverages the nuances of the Vietnamese language to improve classification performance.
Mamba Mamba is an advanced deep learning model designed for efficient NLP tasks. It combines the strengths of RNNs and Transformers, offering high accuracy and fast inference times. Mamba uses a Selective State Space Model (SSM) architecture which allows it to process long sequences effectively, making it particularly suitable for handling complex sentiment analysis tasks.
Bi-LSTM and Bi-GRU These traditional models are used in combination to harness the advantages of both LSTM and GRU architectures, aiming to capture long-term dependencies in text data.
Data Utilized English Dataset SemEval-2014 Task 4: Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis: This dataset contains annotated sentences with aspect terms and corresponding sentiments. It is widely used for benchmarking ABSA models. Vietnamese Dataset Quy Nhon AI Hackathon 2022 - Challenge 2: Review Analytics: This dataset includes reviews from websites and forums about tourism in Binh Dinh, annotated with various aspects and sentiments. The dataset is labeled with aspects such as entertainment services, accommodation services, restaurant systems, dining services, transportation services, and shopping services. Data Preprocessing Cleaning: Removing punctuation and special characters to standardize the text. Tokenization: Splitting text into individual tokens using whitespace as delimiters. Aspect Labeling: Annotating text with specific aspects for sentiment analysis. Normalization: Converting text to lowercase and standardizing formats to ensure consistency across the dataset. Evaluation The evaluation of the models was based on the following metrics:
Accuracy: The proportion of correctly classified instances among the total instances. Precision: The ratio of correctly predicted positive observations to the total predicted positives. Recall: The ratio of correctly predicted positive observations to all observations in the actual class. F1 Score: The weighted average of Precision and Recall. Results and Analysis The results show that:
Mamba achieved the highest accuracy in Vietnamese sentiment analysis with an accuracy of 0.87. PhoBERT followed closely with an accuracy of 0.86. BERT-based models performed well in English sentiment analysis with an accuracy of 0.81. Bi-LSTM and Bi-GRU had the lowest performance, indicating traditional models may not be as effective for modern NLP tasks. Model Performance Comparison Model Language Accuracy BERT-based English 0.81 PhoBERT Vietnamese 0.86 Mamba Vietnamese 0.87 Bi-LSTM + Bi-GRU Vietnamese 0.44 Conclusion This project demonstrates the effectiveness of advanced deep learning models in enhancing sentiment analysis for Vietnamese texts. The findings highlight the importance of model selection and data preprocessing in achieving high accuracy in sentiment classification. Mamba and PhoBERT showed significant improvements over traditional models, indicating their suitability for complex NLP tasks.

title: Course Condition HUST
content:
Problem Many HUST students are not eligible to take the course in the next semester because they do not complete the required courses. That makes it difficult for students to complete the program on time without following detailed qualifying courses. Keeping track of the conditional course makes students spend a lot of time and can’t observe all of them.
Solution We have put into use this dependent module drawing model to make it easier to track students’ conditional courses.
Operation Source data is downloaded from https://ctt-sis.hust.edu.vn/pub/CourseLists.aspx then analyze the condition information and build the family tree of the modules. Limits: new condition support and, or. Parallel subjects are considered as conditions and.
Usage ver 1: Display all the courses that are dependent on the set of interest. ver 2: Continue tracing to form a full tree branch of dependent modules. ver 3: Displays the course name, number of credits, tuition credits, and weights at the end of the semester. *If no graphs are displayed, the set code does not exist in the database. You can use it at: http://sinno.soict.ai/dkhp.html Prize We were fortunate to get the third prize in the contest “HIGH APPLICATION MVP PRODUCTS CONTEST 2022” organized by SVMC (Samsung Vietnam Mobile R&D Center) and SOICT. This reward is also a motivation for us to develop this product even better

title: Latex Test
content: Giá trị của biểu thức $\sqrt{x^2 + y^2}$ là khoảng cách từ điểm đến gốc tọa độ.
